ticketTitle: Add comprehensive tests in scorer_test.go: unsupported language → N/A, unmeasured complexity not scored as excellent, Swift-like generic unit produces no fabricated dimensions, and regression tests for each fix area.

nonGoals: Do NOT modify production code (scorer.go, pipeline.go, domain/*.go)
Do NOT add GradeNA, Unsupported field, or language_tiers.go
Do NOT create TierGeneric concept or alter Score() signature
Do NOT touch any file other than scorer_test.go
deliverables.objective: Add three directly-compilable tests to scorer_test.go: supported-language regression, no-defaults-injection guard, and unmeasured-complexity bug documentation
deliverables.planText:
Read internal/engine/scorer_test.go (~843 lines) to understand existing test conventions: package engine_test, import set (domain, engine, evidence, policy), helper patterns (evidence.CodeMetrics{...}.ToEvidence(), raw domain.Evidence structs), and assertion style (t.Errorf). Read internal/engine/scorer.go to confirm Score() returns domain.DimensionScores and scoreFromMetrics is unexported. Read internal/domain/dimension.go for Grade enum values (GradeA through GradeF, GradeFromScore). Read internal/domain/record.go for CertificationRecord fields. Read internal/domain/unit.go for Unit/UnitID construction.

Add three new test functions in scorer_test.go:

1. TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks — Regression guard. Construct a Go Unit via domain.NewUnit(domain.NewUnitID("go", "main.go", "main"), domain.UnitTypeFunction). Build realistic evidence: evidence.CodeMetrics{Complexity: 15, Coverage: 0.82, Duplication: 0.03}.ToEvidence() for EvKindCodeMetrics; evidence.GitStats{CommitCount: 42, ChurnRate: 0.12}.ToEvidence() for EvKindGitHistory; evidence.LintResult with Passed=true. Call engine.CertifyUnit(unit, []domain.PolicyRule{}, ev, domain.ExpiryConfig{}, time.Now()). Assert record.Grade is not the zero value (i.e., a real grade was assigned); record.Status is not the zero value; WeightedAverage of record.DimensionScores is > 0. Use the existing domain.DefaultDimensionWeights for the weighted-average check.

2. TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected — Guard against 0.5 default injection. Construct evidence for a generic-like unit: a Go unit (no TierGeneric exists yet) with only one evidence kind (e.g., structural only, no code metrics). Call engine.Score(ev, policy.EvaluationResult{}). Iterate the returned DimensionScores map and assert no value equals 0.5 (the former default). Assert that dimensions without evidence are simply absent from the map (len(scores) equals only the dimensions backed by evidence).

3. TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScoredExcellent — Document the complexity=0 bug. Construct evidence with complexity=0 (simulating what a Swift/generic file would produce when no complexity analyzer runs). Use raw domain.Evidence{Kind: domain.EvidenceKindCodeMetrics, Source: "generic", Passed: true, Metrics: map[string]float64{"complexity": 0, "coverage": 0}}. Call engine.Score(ev, policy.EvaluationResult{}). Assert that the maintainability dimension (domain.DimMaintainability) is either absent from the returned map OR its value is not 0.95. This test will CURRENTLY FAIL because scoreFromMetrics in scorer.go sets maintainability to 0.95 when complexity <= 5 — this is the bug being documented. Add a comment above the test: "BUG(iksnae/code-certification#25): scoreFromMetrics incorrectly assigns excellent (0.95) maintainability when complexity=0 for unmeasured languages."

Run: go test ./internal/engine/... -count=1. Expect TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks and TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected to PASS. Expect TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScoredExcellent to FAIL (documenting the bug). Run go test ./... -count=1 for full-project verification.

nonGoals: Do NOT add domain.GradeNA — use current Grade enum only
Do NOT add Unsupported field to CertificationRecord
Do NOT create aggregate functions in scorer.go
Do NOT touch files outside scorer_test.go
deliverables.objective: Add two skipped stub tests for unsupported-language N/A and aggregate-skip behavior, compilable against current types
deliverables.planText:
These tests document behavior expected after issue #25 (language tiers, GradeNA, Unsupported field) is implemented. They must compile against the CURRENT API surface but use t.Skip to avoid failing CI.

Read internal/domain/dimension.go for current Grade enum, internal/domain/record.go for CertificationRecord struct, internal/engine/pipeline.go for CertifyUnit signature.

Add two test functions in scorer_test.go:

1. TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA — Stub for post-#25 behavior. Construct a Swift-like Unit: domain.NewUnit(domain.NewUnitID("swift", "lib.swift", "doWork"), domain.UnitTypeFunction). Call engine.CertifyUnit with empty evidence. Use t.Skip("Requires issue #25: GradeNA and Unsupported field not yet added to domain types") as the first line. After the skip, document the expected assertions as comments: // EXPECTED after #25: record.Grade == domain.GradeNA (or GradeN/A), record.Unsupported == true, len(record.DimensionScores) == 0.

2. TestScore_AggregateSkipsUnsupported — Stub for post-#25 behavior. Construct two units: one Go (supported), one Swift (unsupported). Call engine.CertifyUnit for each. Use t.Skip("Requires issue #25: no aggregate function or unsupported-language skipping in CertifyUnit yet"). Document expected assertions: // EXPECTED after #25: Go unit gets a real grade (A-F), Swift unit is skipped or returns N/A, aggregate result excludes the unsupported unit.

Run: go test ./internal/engine/... -count=1. Both new tests should compile and show as SKIP. The three tests from the previous deliverable should still pass (or fail for the documented bug). Run go test ./... -count=1 for full-project verification.
